works.title: Turning the Morning Bake Into Melbourne's Most-Tagged Bakery
works.description: A family-run sourdough bakery with incredible product but zero online presence. We built a daily content ritual that made Golden Crust the most tagged bakery in Fitzroy.
works.challenge: Golden Crust is a husband-and-wife bakery making some of Melbourne's best sourdough — but nobody outside the neighbourhood knew it existed. With 800 followers and no content strategy, they were invisible to the thousands of people searching for bakeries in the inner north every week.
works.approach:
We designed a content system around what they already did beautifully: bake. Every morning, we filmed 60 seconds of the process — the dough stretch, the scoring, the oven pull. This became 'The Morning Bake', a daily Reels series that turned routine into ritual.

We paired this with a hyperlocal hashtag strategy and optimised their Google Business profile so discovery happened both on Instagram and in search. The content felt authentic because it was — no staging, no scripts, just real craft.
works.impact: What started as a content experiment became the engine of their business growth. The consistency of daily posting built a loyal audience that didn't just watch — they visited.
results: Hired 2 additional staff to meet demand

works.title: Building a Waitlist Before the Doors Even Opened
works.description: A reformer Pilates studio that needed to go from zero to fully booked before opening day. We built anticipation through teaser content and a UGC-driven community strategy.
works.challenge: Studio Raw was a brand-new reformer Pilates studio opening in South Yarra with no social presence, no email list, and no marketing budget beyond our retainer. The founder needed classes filled from day one — there was no runway for a slow build.
works.approach:
We started 8 weeks before launch with a two-phase strategy. Phase one was all anticipation: behind-the-scenes TikToks of the studio build-out, reformer installations, and aesthetic details that made people want to be part of it.

Phase two was conversion: a founding members campaign with early bird pricing, promoted through Instagram Stories with a referral incentive that turned every sign-up into a recruiter.

Post-launch, we shifted to a UGC engine — every member who posted a Story tagging Studio Raw got featured on the main account, creating a self-sustaining content loop.
works.impact: Studio Raw launched to a waitlist, not an empty room. The UGC strategy meant the community was creating content for us within weeks — content we then repurposed into paid ads.
results: 45 organic UGC posts in first month

works.title: One TikTok. 2.4 Million Views. Sold Out for 10 Weeks.
works.description: A 30-seat CBD laneway dumpling bar with no online presence. We focused entirely on TikTok — and one video of their chilli oil dumplings changed everything.
works.challenge: Momo is a tiny dumpling bar tucked into a CBD laneway. The food is exceptional — handmade dumplings, house chilli oil, bamboo steamer service — but nobody could find them. The owner had never posted on social media and didn't know where to start.
works.approach:
We went all-in on TikTok because the food was inherently cinematic. Close-up shots of handmade dumplings, chilli oil drizzles, and steam rising from bamboo baskets — no styling needed, just good angles and trending audio.

We posted 4-5 times per week, testing different formats. The breakthrough was a 15-second slow-motion clip of their signature chilli oil dumplings being pulled apart. We boosted it with just $50 in TikTok Spark Ads to give it initial velocity.
works.impact: That single video hit 2.4 million views and fundamentally changed Momo's business. What was once a quiet laneway spot became a destination — and the ongoing content keeps new customers coming every week.
results: Sold out every Fri & Sat for 10 weeks straight
$50 ad spend sparked the viral moment

works.title: From Weekend Markets to 18K Followers and 180% Online Sales Growth
works.description: A two-person streetwear label selling at markets but struggling online. We repositioned their content around the maker story and turned followers into buyers.
works.challenge: Thread & Co is a small streetwear label run by two designers out of a Collingwood warehouse. They had strong market-day sales but couldn't translate that energy online. Their Instagram felt flat — polished product shots that looked like every other brand — and it wasn't driving DTC sales.
works.approach:
We killed the polished aesthetic and went raw. Instead of product-on-white, we focused on behind-the-scenes content: screen printing process, fabric sourcing trips, packing orders at 2am before a drop. The story of making became the product.

We created a consistent cadence — 4 feed posts and 10+ Stories per week — and introduced 'Drop Day' as a TikTok content format, building anticipation for each release with countdowns, sneak peeks, and live packing sessions.
works.impact: Thread & Co's audience went from passive scrollers to invested insiders. The engagement rate tells the story — 4.2% is exceptional for fashion, and it converts because the audience feels ownership in the brand.
results: Every drop sells out within 48 hours

works.title: Launching a Skincare Brand With 15 Creators and a 2.6x ROAS
works.description: A small-batch natural skincare brand with a Shopify store and no traffic. We ran a dual-track launch — influencer seeding plus Meta ads — that delivered returns from month one.
works.challenge: Bloom Skincare is a two-person operation making natural, small-batch products from a studio in Brunswick. They'd built a beautiful Shopify store but had almost no traffic, no social following, and no idea how to launch into a crowded skincare market on social media.
works.approach:
We ran a two-track launch strategy. Track one was influencer seeding: 15 carefully selected nano creators (5K-30K followers) in skincare and wellness received product in exchange for honest reviews. No scripts, no mandatories — just real people trying real products.

Track two was performance: Michelle built a Meta ads funnel using the UGC these creators produced. We tested 12 different creative variations, found the top three performers within two weeks, and scaled spend behind them.
works.impact: Bloom proved that you don't need a massive budget to launch on social — you need the right creators and a disciplined testing framework. Three creator posts went organically viral, generating earned media value that far exceeded the seeding cost.
results: 2.6x ROAS on Meta ads from month one

works.title: How a Corner Cafe Became Richmond's Community Hub on Instagram
works.description: A neighbourhood cafe lost in Melbourne's crowded coffee scene. We built a community-first content strategy that told human stories — and doubled their weekday covers.
works.challenge: Little Lane is a corner cafe in Richmond with great coffee and a beautiful space, but it was invisible online. The owner felt like she was competing against hundreds of identical Melbourne cafes and couldn't break through. Every cafe was posting the same latte art and avo toast.
works.approach:
We differentiated through people, not product. Instead of food content, we told the human stories: the regular who's been coming since day one, the barista's journey from home brewing to competition, the local artist whose work hangs on the walls.

We also launched a weekly 'Local's Pick' Story series where followers voted on the next special menu item — turning the audience into collaborators, not just consumers.
works.impact: Little Lane stopped competing on coffee and started competing on community. The story-driven approach caught the attention of Broadsheet Melbourne, who featured them as one of the city's best neighbourhood cafes — editorial coverage no ad budget could buy.
